provisions.content: This Order may be cited as the Public Recreation Grounds Order.

Each of the areas of public land described in the Schedule has been declared to be a public recreation ground for the purposes of the Public Recreation Grounds Act. Schedule Arusha Municipality All that piece of land being 6.88 acres in extent shown delineated in red on Plan D. 5 92/1 Registered No. 10230, deposited with the Ministry of Lands 1 . 1 G.N. No. 154 of 1958 All that area of land within the Municipality of Arusha amounting to 4.5 acres and contained by a succession of straight line boundaries commencing at survey beacon EB 479 then passing in turn through survey beacons EB 477, EB 475, EB 474, EB 444, EB 496, EB 495 and EB 494; thence, in the direction of survey beacon EB 493, to the furrow; thence by a curvilinear boundary following the furrow to its intersection with the straight line joining survey beacons EB 479 and EB 480; thence by a straight line to survey beacon EB 479, more particularly defined as the area edged blue on registered plan No. 9794, deposited with the Ministry of Lands 2 . 2 G.N. No. 236 of 1962 All that area of land within the Municipality of Arusha amounting to 3.089 acres, contained by a succession of straight line boundaries commencing at survey beacon EB 440, then passing in turn through survey beacons EB 441, EB 442, EC 969, EC 970, EB 862 and thence to survey beacon EB 440, more particularly defined as the area edged green on registered plan No. 11833, deposited with the Survey Division of the Ministry of Lands. All that area of land within the Municipality of Arusha amounting to 3.945 acres, contained by a succession of straight line boundaries commencing at survey beacon EB 461, then passing in turn through survey beacons V 920, V 919, EB 467, V 915, EB 466, EB 469, EB 471, EB 472, EA 772, EA 771, EB 448, EA 865, EB 447, EB 438, EB 437 and thence to survey beacon EB 461, more particularly defined as the area edged in blue on registered plan No. 13399, deposited with the Ministry of Lands 3 . 3 G.N. No. 56 of 1966 Bukoba Town All that area of land amounting to 10.43 acres contained by a succession of straight line boundaries commencing at boundary beacon V.92, then passing in turn through boundary beacons DB639, V.85, V.84, V.37, GD.238, GD.239, DB.95, DB.18, 27SE, 27NE, DA.2, 32SW, 32NW, D.1221, D.1220, 41NE, V.90, V.91, and thence to V.92, more particularly defined as the area edged blue on Registered Plan No. 9465, deposited with the Ministry of Lands 4 . 4 G.N. No. 249 of 1964 Dar es salaam City All that piece or parcel of land situated at Mnazi Mmoja between Kisarawe Street and Bagamoyo Street and shown coloured green on Plan No. D'223/E (Registered Plan No. 13381), deposited with the Director, Department of Lands and Surveys 5 . 5 G.N.s. Nos. 55 of 1955; 6 of 1959; 18 of 1965; 496 of 1965 All that area of land comprising approximately one hundred and three acres shown edged in green excluding the roads marked in red, the area of the Gymkhana Club edged in blue and the Pumping Station site coloured brown on the Registered Plan No. 11040 and also excluding 6 — 6 G.N.s. Nos. 376 of 1959; 149 of 1971; 363 of 1992 (a) all that piece of land in the Golf Course Area of Ghana Avenue, Dar es Salaam City being approximately 10.89 acres in extent and bounded by beacons AP. 826-AP. 831 AQ. 160, AP. 832, AG. 159, AP. 833, AP. 834, CB. 6, CB. 7, AK. 554, AK. 553, AR. 591, AK. 552 and AP. 851, the whole as delineated on Registered Plan No. 14953; (b) the Areas of the "Nyumba ya Sanaa", Danish Embassy and the Sheraton Hotel Site as shown respectively on Plan No. D'583, D'588/5 and D'336/4 (Registered Plan No. 25514) all deposited at the Office of the Director of Surveys and Mapping Division of the Ministry of Lands. The land contained by a succession of straight line boundaries commencing at boundary beacon No. AH485 then passing in turn through boundary beacons AH486, AH510, AH513 and then to AH485 as delineated in red and marked "Public Recreation Ground No. 1" on registered plan No. 12870, deposited with the Survey Division of the Ministry of Lands 7 . 7 G.N. No. 479 of 1964 The land contained by a succession of straight line boundaries commencing at boundary beacon No. AH480 then passing in turn through boundary beacons AH481, AH491, IPC"A", IPC"B", IPC"C", IPC"D", IPC"E", IPC"F", AH490, AH489, AH492 and thence to AH480, as delineated in red and marked "Public Recreation Ground No. 2" on registered plan No. 12870. Note- The effect of this order is to excise from the Mnazi Mmoja public recreation ground land required for a Nursery School and the National Assembly Building 8 . 8 G.N. No. 18 of 1965 Dodoma Municipality All that piece or parcel of land approximately 9.65 acres in extent situated at Dodoma and contained within survey beacons HD.50, HD.54, HD.53, HD.52 and HD.51, delineated in red on Registered Plan No. 10402, held by the Chief Surveyor, Ministry of Lands, Dar es Salaam 9 . 9 G.N. No. 350 of 1959 Iringa Municipality excluding all roads passing through the area 10 10 G.N. No. 93 of 1956 All that piece or parcel of land situate at Iringa estimated to contain 10.5 acres more or less and bounded as follows— Commencing at a point on the eastern side of Kingsway approximately 870 feet northwards from the north-west corner of Plot 97; thence in a straight line at right angles to Kingsway to a point 60 feet from the western boundary of Dodoma Road; thence southwards for 900 feet by a straight line parallel to Dodoma Road; thence westwards at right angles to Dodoma Road to the eastern side of the road reserve which runs from Plots 87-96; thence northwards and westwards by that side of the road reserve to the eastern side of Kingsway; thence northwards by the eastern side of Kingsway to the point of commencement; as delineated on Registered Plan No. CTPO. 256, held by the Chief Surveyor, Department of Lands and Surveys, Dar es Salaam. Commencing at survey beacon No. X.889, which marks the south-east corner of the junction of Kingsway and Pawaga Road in Iringa Municipality the boundary runs on a bearing of 60 degrees 59 minutes and 59 seconds for a distance of 267.34 feet to survey beacon No. J.581; thence on a bearing of 128 degrees 12 minutes and 29 seconds for a distance of 39.22 feet to beacon No. R. 1497; thence on a bearing of 150 degrees 41 minutes and 03 seconds for a distance of 199.52 feet to beacon No. J.594; thence on a bearing of 189 degrees 22 minutes and 37 seconds for a distance of 72.85 feet to beacon No. J.587; thence on a bearing of 217 degrees 00 minutes and 18 seconds for a distance of 292.54 feet to beacon No. G.1694; thence on a bearing of 300 degrees 26 minutes and 36 seconds for a distance of 65.87 feet to beacon No. G.1693; thence on a bearing of 311 degree 01 minute and 57 seconds for a distance of 60.81 feet to beacon No. G.1692; thence on a bearing of 312 degrees 35 minutes and 33 seconds for a distance of 61.99 feet to beacon No. G.1691; thence on a bearing of 343 degrees 38 minutes and 14 seconds for a distance of 203.03 feet to beacon No. X.890; thence on a bearing of 22 degrees 30 minutes and 03 seconds for a distance of 62.20 feet to the point of commencement 11 . 11 G.N. No. 165 of 1960 All bearings are referred to True North. The boundary is shown edged in red on Plan No. D.18.66, deposited with the Survey Division of the Ministry of Lands, Dar es Salaam. Kilosa Town Commencing at point "A", which demarcates the south-east corner of the P.W.D. depot plot and lies on a bearing of approximately 94 degrees 54 minutes at a distance of approximately 360 feet from beacon K91, which is the south-east beacon of petrol godown plot 13; thence on a bearing of approximately 94 degrees 54 minutes for a distance of approximately 535 feet to point "B"; thence in a northerly direction for a distance of approximately 600 feet, parallel to point "C"; thence directly to the said road reserve boundary, for a distance of approximately 65 feet so that it joins the said road reserve boundary in a right angle at point "D"; thence following the said road reserve boundary, in a northerly direction for approximately 100 feet to its intersection with the south bank of a watercourse at point "E"; thence along the south bank of the said watercourse in a westerly direction for a distance of approximately 600 feet to point "F"; thence on a bearing of approximately 274 degrees 54 minutes for a distance of approximately 400 feet to point "G"; which demarcates the north-east corner of the aforementioned P.W.D. depot; thence continuing on this bearing for 200 feet to point "A", the point of commencement; the whole as shown in green on Registered Plan CTPO. 374, deposited in the Department of Lands and Surveys, Dar es Salaam 12 . 12 G.N. No. 415 of 1957 Lindi Town Commencing at the north-eastern corner of the junction of Market Avenue and Artillery Road, thence northwards along the eastern boundary of Artillery Road to the southern boundary of the government quarters plot; thence eastwards along the southern boundaries of the government quarters, school and cemetery plots to the western boundary of Coast Street; thence southwards along the western boundary of Coast Street to a point in line with the northern boundary of the Hindu Mandal plot; thence to the north-eastern beacon of the Hindu Mandal plot; thence following the northern, western and southern boundaries of the Hindu Mandal Plot to its south-eastern beacon; thence crossing Coast Street to the south-western beacon of Plot 31 "H"; thence along the southern boundary of Plot 31 "H" to the north-western beacon of Plot 30 "H"; thence southwards and eastwards along the eastern and southern boundaries of Plot 30 to Marine Avenue; thence southwards along the western boundary of Marine Avenue to a point being the intersection of a line drawn parallel to and a 100 feet from the northern boundary of plot 27 "H" the Police lines; thence westwards along the said line parallel to Plot 27 "H" for a distance of 450 feet; thence due south to the northern boundary of the cemetery; thence north-eastwards crossing Coast Street to the western side of Coast Street; thence southwards along the western boundary of Coast Street to the northern boundary of Market Street; thence north-westwards along the northern boundary of Market Street to its junction with Artillery Road the point of commencement; as shown edged in red on Registered Plan No. 11275, deposited with the Survey Division of the Ministry of Lands, Dar es Salaam 13 . 13 G.N. No. 164 of 1960 Mbeya Municipality All that area of land amounting to 13.61 acres contained by a succession of straight line boundaries commencing at boundary beacon IA.820, then passing in turn through boundary beacons J.1490, J.1491, J.1492, IA.813, IA.832, IA.831, IA.830, IA.829, IA.828, IA.827, IA.826, IA.825, IA.824, IA.823, IA.822, IA.821, and thence to IA.820 more particularly defined as the area edged red on registered plan No. 12161, deposited with the Survey Division of the Ministry of Lands, Dar es Salaam 14 . 14 G.N. No. 495 of 1964 Morogoro Municipality King George VI Memorial Stadium, Morogoro: Commencing at survey beacon B.247; thence south-eastwards for 185.9 feet through survey beacon B.248 to B.249; thence southwards for 575.9 feet through survey beacons B.241, B.242 and B.245 to B.243; thence westwards for 193.4 feet to survey beacon B.244; thence northwards for 583.6 feet through survey beacons B.238, B.237 and B.246 to B.251; thence north-eastwards for 178.0 feet through survey beacon B.250 to B.247, the point of commencement, the whole enclosing 4.285 acres as delineated and coloured green on Survey Plan D. 13/143 registered and held by the Chief Surveyor, Lands and Surveys Department, Dar es Salaam 15 . 15 G.N. No. 417 of 1955 Moshi Municipality Commencing at the point where the southern boundary of the new Namanga-Taveta road reserve crosses the eastern bank of the Karanga River; thence eastwards along the southern side of that road reserve for ap
